codeReview.summary: The TPIDR-CHAIN.md document is a thorough and accurate cross-module invariant verification. All 6 write sites and 4 read sites enumerated in the document match the actual kernel source (confirmed via grep across all 35 .rs + 2 .S files). Code snippets are accurate. The Fix 17 soundness analysis is correct — the no-nomem constraint on the asm block prevents LLVM reordering past the lock release, and the document clearly explains the original race window and why the fix closes it. The structural foundation section (Thread layout, Context at offset 0, Box stability, PerCpu separation) is verified against source. The boot timeline correctly traces the init-to-steady-state sequence for both core 0 and secondary cores. The boot.S omission is confirmed correct (no TPIDR_EL1 references in boot.S, no exception sources enabled before scheduler::init). The invariant violation table covers the key failure modes. Three line number references in scheduler.rs are inaccurate (off by ~20 lines) but non-blocking since function names and code snippets are correct.

issues.description: Write 1 line reference claims 'scheduler.rs:1026–1033' but the SAFETY comment + asm block is at scheduler.rs:1045–1054 (off by ~19 lines). The code snippet itself is accurate — only the line numbers are wrong.

Write 2 line reference claims 'scheduler.rs:1054–1059' but the SAFETY comment + asm block is at scheduler.rs:1074–1081 (off by ~20 lines). The code snippet itself is accurate — only the line numbers are wrong.
Write 3 line reference claims 'scheduler.rs:412–429' but the full comment + asm block spans lines 416–434. The range is slightly narrow — the asm block extends 5 lines beyond the claimed end. Minor.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The kernel-auditor skill's Step 3 says to add '// AUDIT:' comments per file audited. For cross-module invariant features, the worker correctly produced a standalone document instead. The skill's 'Special Cases' section mentions 'create a document or code comments mapping the invariants' for cross-file features, but the main procedure still implies per-file AUDIT comments. The worker's skillFeedback.suggestedChanges correctly flags this ambiguity.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill SKILL.md Step 3 says 'add an // AUDIT: comment at the top of the file'. Special Cases section says 'trace specific cross-cutting concerns across all relevant files. Create a document or code comments mapping the invariants.' Worker handoff skillFeedback: 'the skill could clarify this distinction.'

summary: PASS. The TPIDR_EL1 invariant chain document is comprehensive and accurate. All 6 write sites and 4 read sites are correctly enumerated and verified against source. Fix 17 soundness analysis is correct. Three scheduler.rs line number references are off by ~20 lines (non-blocking — function names and code snippets are accurate). The document meets all three expectedBehavior criteria: all write sites enumerated, invariant verified at each write, and Fix 17 defense-in-depth confirmed sound.
